prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: condensed Fukui functions (f+, f−, f0, dual) for each atom in ferrocene; most electrophilic site’s f+ value reported here.
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding) implemented in xtb-6.6.  No Gaussian basis set; no density-fitting approximation.
Geometry provenance: single-point calculation on the user-supplied experimental geometry file “ferrocene.xyz”; no re-optimization performed (GFN2-xTB//user geometry).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge 0, multiplicity 1 for the neutral parent; the N–1 and N+1 states were treated as doublets (charge +1/−1, multiplicity 2) as per the skill default.
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (engine subcommand “fukui” calling xtb 6.6).
Convergence & warnings: SCF cycles for the neutral, cation, and anion all converged; integrity status "ok", trustworthy = true.  The engine returned three warnings, reproduced verbatim below.
Live log & files: the run streamed live output to a “fukui_<timestamp>.out” log (path was printed when the job launched); the full JSON result is at /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpzivfxsbm/live.json and the bar-chart PNG is at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/argo_o3/20260704-152640_ferrocene_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png.
